name: Too Real
short_description: A short, hand-drawn narrative about memory, loss, and final conversations. Explore the quiet streets of the past and decide what you're ready to let go.
detailed_description:
What would you do if someone you lost suddenly returned as if nothing had ever happened?

In this short hand-drawn narrative adventure with point-and-click exploration, you follow a girl Yume as she revisits a quiet neighborhood from her past, the place where she once lived with Noah, the partner who mysteriously disappeared years ago.

There were no explanations, no goodbye. Time passed, and life slowly moved forward.

But today, as Yume walks those familiar streets again, Noah suddenly appears, as if the years in between never existed.

Together, they begin to talk about all the questions that were never answered.

As you explore the environment and guide their conversations, your choices shape how Yume confronts what happened. Will she hold onto the past, searching for answers, or accept the truth and move forward?

Some memories are comforting.
Some are painful.
And some may not be memories at all.

Features

  • Branching dialogue with meaningful choices

  • Hidden objects and story clues to discover

  • Several endings shaped by your decisions

  • Hand-drawn art and an immersive original soundtrack

  • Deep, emotional narrative

  • Developed solo with care and attention to detail

  • Available in English, Russian, and Japanese

reviews.review_text: Absolutely love the atmosphere of this psychological artwork. I wish there'd be a sequel to the story! I also hope the developer adds extras with game's audio tracks to enjoy them at any time.

The developer put a lot of personal feelings into this game.
Music is immersive and fits the atmosphere perfectly.
